moving-checklist.intro: Moving from South Africa to the Netherlands involves long-haul logistics, document preparation, and money transfer planning. This checklist helps you cover pre-departure steps, travel prep, and a clear handoff to your first days in the Netherlands.
whatOftenMatters: Long-haul move: book flights and shipping well in advance
Document prep timing: allow for DHA and apostille where needed
Money transfer and banking: plan for first-month costs and possible delays in opening a Dutch account
School and family documents if moving with children
documentConsiderations: South African civil records may need apostille; allow time for DHA and logistics
Passport validity and renewal: avoid last-minute renewal
transferTravelNotes: Consider currency exchange and transfer services for initial setup costs
Keep essential documents in hand luggage

arrival-planner.intro: If you're arriving in the Netherlands from South Africa, your first days will focus on registration, BSN, and banking. This planner helps you sequence those steps and plan for the buffer you may need after a long-haul move.
whatOftenMatters: Allow a short buffer after long-haul arrival before heavy admin
Document accessibility: have originals and certified copies ready
Early sequencing: municipality registration → BSN → bank account → other setup
documentConsiderations: Have passport and any required certified or apostilled documents to hand for registration

first-90-days.intro: Your first 90 days from South Africa will include stabilizing banking, transport, and routines. This planner covers week 2 onward: DigiD, GP, recurring payments, and integration choices.
whatOftenMatters: Transfer and banking continuity from your existing setup
Driving licence awareness if you plan to drive in the Netherlands

document-readiness.intro: South African document authorities (e.g. DHA for civil records, passport) can have variable processing times. This checker helps you see what you need and plan for apostille or translation where required.
whatOftenMatters: Civil records (birth, marriage) from DHA; apostille may be required
Passport validity and renewal lead times
documentConsiderations: Apostille and legalization for certain civil documents
Translation of documents if not in English or Dutch

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from the United States to the Netherlands requires careful prep: visa/permit route, document timing, and travel logistics. This checklist covers pre-move steps, money transfer, and handoff to arrival.
whatOftenMatters: Visa or residence permit route (e.g. highly skilled migrant, DAFT)
Document certification and apostille where needed
Money transfer and first-month costs in euros
documentConsiderations: U.S. civil documents: state-issued certified copies; apostille may be required
Passport validity for the intended stay

arrival-planner.intro: After arriving from the U.S., your first days in the Netherlands will center on registration, BSN, and banking. This planner helps you prioritize and sequence those steps.
whatOftenMatters: Municipality registration and BSN as first priorities
Health insurance within the required period

first-90-days.intro: Your first 90 days in the Netherlands will include DigiD, GP registration, and daily life setup. This planner helps you stabilize routines and admin after your move from the U.S.

document-readiness.intro: U.S. documents (passport, civil records, employment papers) often need to be in order before and after your move. This checker helps you see what's required and plan for apostille or translation.
whatOftenMatters: Passport and copies for registration and banking

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from India to the Netherlands involves visa/permit preparation, document attestation, and travel planning. This checklist covers pre-departure steps and handoff to your first days.
whatOftenMatters: Visa and permit route and employer sponsorship docs
Family documents if moving with dependants
documentConsiderations: Passport and civil documents; attestation and apostille as required
Educational and employment documents for permit and registration

arrival-planner.intro: Your first days in the Netherlands from India will focus on registration, BSN, and banking. This planner helps you sequence admin and avoid common bottlenecks.
whatOftenMatters: Registration and BSN as soon as you have an address
Bank account for salary and payments
Document originals and copies ready for appointments

first-90-days.intro: Stabilizing your first 90 days after moving from India includes DigiD, GP, and daily routines. This planner covers week 2 to month 3.

document-readiness.intro: Indian document requirements for the Netherlands can include attestation and apostille. This checker helps you see what to prepare and where to get it.
whatOftenMatters: Passport and civil documents; MEA attestation where applicable
Translation if documents are not in English or Dutch
documentConsiderations: Apostille/legalization and translation awareness

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from Germany to the Netherlands is often straightforward for EU citizens, but document and housing prep still matter. This checklist covers pre-move steps and handoff to arrival.

arrival-planner.intro: Your first days in the Netherlands from Germany will focus on registration, BSN, and banking. This planner helps you sequence those steps.

first-90-days.intro: Your first 90 days in the Netherlands will include DigiD, GP, and daily life setup. This planner helps you stabilize after your move from Germany.

document-readiness.intro: German documents (ID, civil records) need to be in order for your move to the Netherlands. This checker helps you see what's required.

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from Switzerland to the Netherlands is usually simpler on immigration than non-EU moves: Swiss citizens are covered in IND guidance alongside EU and EEA nationals. Housing, registration, BSN, and insurance still need real planning. This checklist covers pre-move steps and handoff to arrival.
documentConsiderations: Netherlands Worldwide Switzerland page: some documents immediate, others need apostille

arrival-planner.intro: Your first days from Switzerland typically focus on gemeente registration, BSN, banking, and insurance—not MVV steps for the ordinary Swiss route.

first-90-days.intro: Your first 90 days will include DigiD, GP, and daily routines. This planner helps you stabilize after your move from Switzerland.

document-readiness.intro: Some Swiss documents can be used directly in the Netherlands; others need a Swiss apostille per Netherlands Worldwide. This checker helps you plan before gemeente or employer submissions.
documentConsiderations: Verify each document with the requesting authority

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from Sweden to the Netherlands as an EU citizen means freedom of movement: no visa or standard residence permit for ordinary moves. Housing, gemeente registration, BSN, banking, and Dutch basic insurance still need a solid plan.
documentConsiderations: EU documents are often simpler; confirm edge cases via Netherlands Worldwide

arrival-planner.intro: Your first days from Sweden usually centre on registration, BSN, banking, and insurance—like other EU origins.

first-90-days.intro: Your first 90 days include DigiD, GP, and daily routines after your move from Sweden.

document-readiness.intro: Swedish EU documents are usually straightforward for Dutch procedures; unusual document types may still need legalisation per Netherlands Worldwide.

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from Denmark to the Netherlands as an EU citizen means freedom of movement: no visa or standard residence permit for ordinary moves. Plan housing, gemeente registration, BSN within official timelines, banking, and Dutch basic insurance.
documentConsiderations: Netherlands Worldwide Denmark page: e-apostille for many diplomas; sworn translation for Danish unless multilingual form applies

arrival-planner.intro: First days from Denmark focus on registration, BSN, banking, and insurance—same EU pattern as other nearby origins.

first-90-days.intro: Your first 90 days include DigiD, GP, and daily routines after your move from Denmark.

document-readiness.intro: Danish documents: some immediate use; diplomas and certificates often need e-apostille; Danish usually needs sworn translation into an accepted language unless a standard multilingual form is accepted.

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from Norway to the Netherlands as an EEA national is aligned with EU/EEA stay rules in IND guidance: no MVV-centred path for ordinary moves. Plan housing, gemeente registration within official timelines, BSN, banking, and Dutch basic insurance.
documentConsiderations: Netherlands Worldwide Norway page: some documents immediate; others need apostille—verify per document

arrival-planner.intro: First days from Norway focus on registration, BSN, banking, and insurance—similar practical sequencing to other nearby EEA/EU origins.

first-90-days.intro: Your first 90 days include DigiD, GP, and daily routines after your move from Norway.

document-readiness.intro: Norwegian documents: some can be used directly in the Netherlands; others need apostille per Netherlands Worldwide. Confirm language and translation expectations with each receiving authority.

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from Ireland to the Netherlands as an EU citizen means freedom of movement: no visa or standard residence permit for ordinary moves. Plan housing, gemeente registration within official timelines, BSN, Dutch basic health insurance from arrival (per Government.nl), banking, and practical setup.
documentConsiderations: Netherlands Worldwide Ireland page: some documents immediate; others need Irish apostille—verify per document

arrival-planner.intro: First days from Ireland focus on registration, BSN, banking, and Dutch basic insurance sequencing—same EU pattern as other nearby origins.

first-90-days.intro: Your first 90 days include DigiD, GP, and daily routines after your move from Ireland.

document-readiness.intro: Irish documents: some immediate use in the Netherlands; others need apostille from Irish authorities per Netherlands Worldwide. Foreign qualifications for work may follow separate checks.

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from Türkiye to the Netherlands is immigration-first for long stays: Turkish nationals usually need a residence permit for stays longer than short visits, and many routes also use an MVV. Short-stay Schengen applications go through VFS Global under Netherlands Worldwide guidance—separate from relocation. This checklist covers pre-move steps and handoff to arrival.
whatOftenMatters: MVV and residence permit with your sponsor where applicable
Apostille on Turkish documents when required
Translations and appointment timing in Türkiye
documentConsiderations: Netherlands Worldwide Türkiye pages for Schengen vs MVV
Legalisation page for documents issued in Türkiye

first-90-days.intro: Your first 90 days will include DigiD, GP registration, and daily routines. This planner helps you stabilize after your move from Türkiye.

document-readiness.intro: Some Turkish documents can be used in the Netherlands immediately; others need apostille. Turkish-language documents generally need translation into Dutch, English, French, or German when required. This checker helps you plan your pack before MVV or gemeente steps.

documentConsiderations: Netherlands Worldwide legalisation page for Türkiye
Verify each document with the requesting body

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from Argentina to the Netherlands is immigration-first for long stays: residence permits for recognised purposes and often an MVV, plus MFA digital e-apostille on Argentine documents per Netherlands Worldwide. Short-stay Schengen from Argentina follows separate rules and timing. Argentina is also on the Dutch Working Holiday list—verify conditions on official pages.
whatOftenMatters: MVV and residence permit when required
Buenos Aires embassy appointments and long-haul logistics
documentConsiderations: Netherlands Worldwide Argentina pages for Schengen vs MVV
Civic integration exam abroad when your route lists it

moving-checklist.intro: Moving from Chile to the Netherlands is immigration-first for long stays: residence permits for recognised purposes and often an MVV, plus Hague apostille on Chilean documents per Netherlands Worldwide. Short-stay Schengen from Chile is handled through the Dutch embassy in Santiago—separate from relocation. When an MVV is required, IND and Netherlands Worldwide describe applying for MVV and residence permit together and a limited window after approval to apply for the MVV sticker—confirm live pages.
whatOftenMatters: MVV and residence permit when required
Santiago embassy appointments and long-haul logistics
documentConsiderations: Netherlands Worldwide Chile pages for Schengen vs MVV
Civic integration exam abroad when your route lists it
